title: Remaining Tasks For The First Stable Installable Ehecoatl Version
mainGoal: Close the remaining correctness, stability, and release-proof gaps identified in the latest system reports so Ehecoatl can be treated as the first stable installable version.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Ordered by release risk, where P0 groups unblock live request serving and essential runtime correctness, and P1 groups close the remaining stability and release-proof gaps.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: Only tasks still required before the stable first installable version are included here. Deferred improvements stay outside the release gate.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: This tasklist supersedes the earlier broad first-installable tasklist by using only the newest report in each report/system area and extracting what is still left to do.

supersedes.note: That earlier tasklist was fully worked through. This new tasklist records the remaining issues found by the latest comparison reports after those changes landed.

sourceReports.topic: Process supervision, child lifecycle, restart, shutdown
HTTP request execution pipeline and response flow
Plugin hook model, discovery/loading, bundled plugin consistency
Tenancy routing, tenant controller flow, setup-time tenant scaffold

taskGroups.title: Restore Live HTTP Pipeline Execution
taskGroups.goal: Make the active default HTTP pipeline complete a real request end-to-end so the scaffold tenant can actually serve assets and controller responses.
pointsOfAttention: The latest HTTP report is still blocked, so this group is the main release blocker.
Do not patch only the tenant side. The first active pipeline stages must actually let requests reach the static-file and controller stages.
The result of this group should be a real request successfully reaching `/htm/index.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session`.

tasks.title: Fix default pipeline stage continuation
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure the active first pipeline stage does not stop the request before static assets or controllers can run.

subtasks.title: Make `mid-queue-stage` return `true` on the normal path after queue registration is complete
Keep `false` returns only for intentional short-circuit paths, not for ordinary request progression
Verify that a real request now reaches `local-file-stream-stage` and `tenant-controller-stage`

tasks.title: Align queue RPC names in cached-response coordination
tasks.reportedGoal: Make HTTP cache coordination talk to the actual manager queue API instead of mismatched literal question names.

subtasks.title: Replace literal `enqueue` and `dequeue` usage in `local-file-stream-stage` with the configured queue question names or a shared constant source
Verify cache coordination still works with the queue broker listener names exposed by manager bootstrap
Confirm this change does not break non-cached controller and asset flows

tasks.title: Normalize body-read HTTP failure output
tasks.reportedGoal: Guarantee that body parsing failures produce a valid HTTP response in the active uWS path.

subtasks.title: Replace the current `res.writeStatus(e)` assumption with explicit status and body mapping
Preserve the existing request hooks and abort behavior around body failure handling
Smoke-test malformed and oversized body scenarios to confirm the response is deterministic

taskGroups.title: Complete Session-Aware Tenant Execution
taskGroups.goal: Make session-enabled routes actually carry loaded session state from manager to engine to tenant controller execution.
pointsOfAttention: The scaffold tenant already demonstrates `/session`, so this group should make that route meaningful beyond cookies alone.
This group also closes the current plugin-hook mismatch in the session path.
The session contract should stay explicit: request cookies, loaded sessionData, and persisted updates should all be visible and coherent.

tasks.title: Attach fetched session payload to the engine execution context
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure `manager.getSessionData()` actually populates `executionContext.sessionData` before the pipeline runs.

subtasks.title: Store the result of the session fetch in `executionContext.sessionData` instead of discarding it
Keep session-disabled routes cheap and avoid unnecessary state mutation when session data is not needed
Verify response-side session persistence still uses the updated in-memory session payload

tasks.title: Pass session data into tenant controller execution
tasks.reportedGoal: Make tenant controllers receive loaded session state, not only cookies and route metadata.

subtasks.title: Include `sessionData` in the tenant-controller RPC request payload
Expose `sessionData` in the tenant controller context created by `bootstrap-tenant-app`
Verify the scaffold `/session` route can observe both `sessionId` and the loaded session payload

tasks.title: Reconcile session hook emissions with the declared plugin hook tree
tasks.reportedGoal: Remove the current session-path drift where SessionRouter emits hook names that do not exist in `plugin-hooks.config`.

subtasks.title: Choose one consistent fix: rename the emitted session hooks or extend the declared MANAGER session hook tree
Keep session diagnostics and plugin visibility intact after the rename or hook-tree expansion
Verify there are no remaining undefined hook consumers in the session path

taskGroups.title: Keep Tenant Routing Fresh And Tenant Failures Explicit
taskGroups.goal: Eliminate stale tenant route matches after rescans and make tenant-side controller load failures return deterministic responses.
pointsOfAttention: This group is about long-running correctness, not only first boot.
Tenant rescans should not leave stale route decisions alive longer than necessary.
Tenant-process failures should become explicit response objects, not generic RPC noise.

tasks.title: Invalidate route cache on successful tenant rescans
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure route lookups reflect changed or removed tenant routes immediately after metadata is rebuilt.

subtasks.title: Clear or invalidate `TenancyRouter.localCache` when a scan successfully rebuilds tenant metadata
Verify changed and removed routes are no longer served from stale cache entries after rescan
Keep the current first-scan readiness guarantee intact while adjusting cache invalidation

tasks.title: Normalize tenant controller load failures locally
tasks.reportedGoal: Convert controller load and require failures into explicit tenant response objects before they leave the tenant process.

subtasks.title: Catch `require.resolve` and `require` failures around controller loading inside `bootstrap-tenant-app`
Return a deterministic error response shape instead of relying on generic RPC listener failure wrapping
Verify the engine receives a predictable HTTP fallback for missing or broken controllers

taskGroups.title: Finish Process Supervision Stabilization
taskGroups.goal: Remove the last process-lifecycle race and ambiguous reload end state before calling the runtime stable.
pointsOfAttention: The process report is mostly-good now, so this is a narrow hardening group rather than a broad refactor.
These fixes matter for confidence in long-running installs, not only happy-path boot.
Keep the current launch rollback and coordinated shutdown gains intact.

tasks.title: Remove the supervisor RPC-listener startup race
tasks.reportedGoal: Guarantee the supervisor is ready to observe child state and heartbeat traffic before any child can emit it.

subtasks.title: Move supervisor router setup to an awaited path or add an explicit readiness barrier before child launch begins
Verify early `ready` and `heartbeat` messages from manager and engine cannot race past listener registration
Keep the current RPC routing and launch hook behavior intact after the bootstrap change

tasks.title: Settle failed intentional reloads into a final terminal state
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure the reload branch emits a clear terminal outcome if the relaunch fails after an intentional restart cycle begins.

subtasks.title: Emit a final terminal signal such as `DEAD` when an intentional reload cannot relaunch successfully
Clear reload bookkeeping and health state consistently in that failure branch
Verify `CRASH`, `RESTART`, and terminal state semantics remain distinct after the change

taskGroups.title: Prove The Stable First Installable Release
taskGroups.goal: Run the real setup-to-served-traffic smoke path on the current scaffold and only then mark the release stable.
pointsOfAttention: The code changes above are not enough on their own; this group is the release proof.
Use the current scaffold flow produced by `setup.sh` and `tenant_create`, not a handcrafted local path.
This group should end with a concrete pass/fail record, not just a claim.

tasks.title: Execute the setup-to-traffic smoke path on a fresh runtime
tasks.reportedGoal: Prove the current install/setup flow produces a working scaffold tenant and a safely stoppable runtime.

subtasks.title: Run `setup.sh`, provide a first tenant domain or `localhost`, and let it scaffold the default tenant
Boot the runtime and verify `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` against the scaffold tenant
Verify the runtime shuts down cleanly and supervised children exit safely

tasks.title: Record the release-proof outcome
tasks.reportedGoal: Capture the result of the smoke run and either clear the release gate or identify the next blocking issue.

subtasks.title: Refresh the relevant system reports if the status changes after the fixes and smoke run
Write a final release-readiness report or tasklist follow-up showing whether the stable first installable gate has passed

deferredAfterStable.whyDeferred: Route-cache writeback after manager misses and a dedicated cache-write stage are useful, but they are not required to call the first installable version stable if correctness and smoke routes are already working.
Duplicate-name override policy, unload teardown callbacks, and per-context activation rules matter for a richer plugin ecosystem, but they are not the current release blockers.
Emitting shutdown and crash states directly from children would improve observability, but it is a follow-up once the remaining correctness and release-proof tasks are closed.

releaseGateForFirstStableInstallableVers.description: Ehecoatl should only be called the first stable installable version when the live HTTP pipeline no longer blocks request progression, session-enabled tenant routes receive real session state, tenant rescans stay fresh, supervisor startup and reload edge cases are settled, and the scaffold tenant passes the setup-to-traffic smoke path.
requiredSmokeChecks: Installer can scaffold a first tenant through setup
Runtime boots with plugin scanning enabled
GET /hello returns the scaffold JSON controller response
GET /session returns the scaffold session JSON response
Runtime shuts down cleanly with supervised child cleanup
